Full Guide

14-Day Western Sichuan Nature Route

Sichuan Jiuzhaigou - Huanglong - Mount Siguniang

This handbook is written directly for international visitors. It is not a general sightseeing introduction, but an actionable 14-day nature route guide. Visitors can use it to choose accommodation areas, plan daily sightseeing, connect transport segments, select meals, estimate budgets and handle backup plans. All prices, ratings and schedules are reference information only. Final costs should be checked again according to travel dates on official platforms, 12306, Trip.com, Ctrip, Dianping, hotel front desks or scenic-area ticket counters.

Start planningSee highlights
Booking-Ready Handbook

Complete route planning in one page

This web version preserves the full content of the paid guide while presenting it in a modern, responsive layout for easier reading on desktop and mobile.

Basic Information

This handbook is written directly for international visitors. It is not a general sightseeing introduction, but an actionable 14-day nature route guide. Visitors can use it to choose accommodation areas, plan daily sightseeing, connect transport segments, select meals, estimate budgets and handle backup plans. All prices, ratings and schedules are reference information only. Final costs should be checked again according to travel dates on official platforms, 12306, Trip.com, Ctrip, Dianping, hotel front desks or scenic-area ticket counters.

Basic InformationDetails
Route ThemeHighland lakes, colorful travertine pools, snow-mountain valleys, alpine forests, panda habitat and light-hiking nature route in Sichuan.
Recommended Duration14 days / 13 nights
Route Entry / ExitEnter from Chengdu; leave from Chengdu
Suitable ForFirst-time international visitors to China, nature photographers, families, couples and light-hiking travelers.
Best SeasonsApril-June and September-November. Mid-to-late October is especially good for Jiuzhaigou autumn colors. Summer has stronger water flow but more rain. Winter is quieter but road and weather uncertainty is higher.
DifficultyMedium to high. Jiuzhaigou and Huanglong are high-altitude scenic areas. Mount Siguniang Town, Shuangqiao Valley, Changping Valley and Haizi Valley are also at high elevation, so walking intensity should be controlled.
Exchange Rate UsedUSD 1 approximately RMB 7.2. USD amounts are rounded and for budgeting only.

Designed for first-time international visitors

Route Highlights

Night View of Chengdu’s Landmark Architecture
Autumn Colors of Rhinoceros Lake in Jiuzhaigou
Panoramic View of Huanglong Five-Color Pond
Muni Valley in Songpan
Panoramic View of Mount Siguniang’s Snow Peaks
Wolong Giant Panda Base

1. Route Logic and City / Scenic Area Introduction

This route focuses on the natural landscapes of the Sichuan plateau. It connects Jiuzhaigou’s colorful lakes, Huanglong’s high-altitude travertine pools, Mount Siguniang’s snow-mountain valleys, and Chengdu as the transport and recovery base. The route is not designed around shopping or nightlife. It emphasizes scenery, light hiking, photography, and safe transfers between highland areas.

PlaceCity / Scenic Area IntroductionAccommodation and Travel Advice
ChengduThe provincial capital of Sichuan and the route’s entry/exit city. It works as an arrival buffer, transport hub, dining base and final recovery stop.Stay near Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li, Tianfu Square, Chengdu East Railway Station or the airport. Choose Chengdu East if taking an early train; choose Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li for dining convenience.
JiuzhaigouA UNESCO World Heritage landscape famous for blue-green lakes, travertine waterfalls, autumn forests and snow-mountain reflections. The scenic area has a Y-shaped layout: Rize Valley, Zechawa Valley and Shuzheng Valley.Stay near the entrance / Zhangzha Town. Two days inside the park are more comfortable than one day, especially for photographers and international visitors.
HuanglongKnown for high-altitude travertine pools, Five-Color Pond, Golden Sand Pavement, snow-mountain views and forests. It is higher and more physically demanding than Jiuzhaigou.Visit during the day from Jiuzhaigou or Chuanzhusi / Songpan. Use the cableway and shuttle combination if energy is limited.
Chuanzhusi / SongpanA key transport and overnight node between Jiuzhaigou, Huanglong and Huanglongjiuzhai Railway Station. Songpan Ancient Town can be used as a short stop, while Chuanzhusi is more practical as a transport base.Use this area as a one-night altitude buffer after Huanglong. Choose hotels with heating, hot water and oxygen support if available.
Mount SiguniangPart of the Sichuan Giant Panda Sanctuaries World Heritage context. The main tourist valleys are Shuangqiao Valley, Changping Valley and Haizi Valley.Stay in Mount Siguniang Town / Rilong Town. Shuangqiao Valley is best for most visitors; Changping Valley is for light hiking; Haizi Valley is better for photography and stronger walkers.
Wolong / Dujiangyan CorridorThe overland corridor between Chengdu and Mount Siguniang, passing Dujiangyan, Yingxiu, Wolong and Balang Mountain areas.Use mainly as a transfer route. Unless time is generous, do not add too many stops on long transfer days.

2. 14-Day Route Overview

DayRouteMain ExperienceOvernight
Day 1Arrive in ChengduArrival, rest, payment and network checkChengdu
Day 2Chengdu buffer and light nature dayPanda Base / city supplies / rail preparationChengdu
Day 3Chengdu East - Huanglongjiuzhai Station - JiuzhaigouHigh-speed rail into northwest Sichuan and rest near the park entranceJiuzhaigou entrance
Day 4Jiuzhaigou core routeRize Valley + Zechawa Valley + NuorilangJiuzhaigou entrance
Day 5Second day in JiuzhaigouShuzheng Valley, repeat Five-Flower Lake, photography or lower-intensity routeJiuzhaigou entrance
Day 6Jiuzhaigou - Huanglong - Chuanzhusi / SongpanHuanglong pools, Five-Color Pond and travertine landscapeChuanzhusi / Songpan
Day 7Chuanzhusi / Songpan - ChengduReturn to Chengdu to reduce altitude and transfer fatigueChengdu
Day 8Chengdu - Mount Siguniang TownLong mountain-road transfer and altitude adaptationMount Siguniang Town
Day 9Shuangqiao ValleySnow mountains, alpine lakes, meadows and redwood forest by sightseeing busMount Siguniang Town
Day 10Changping ValleyForest, streams, boardwalks and light hikingMount Siguniang Town
Day 11Haizi Valley / Maobiliang / RestSnow-mountain viewpoints, short hiking or recoveryMount Siguniang Town
Day 12Mount Siguniang Town - ChengduReturn to Chengdu and recoverChengdu
Day 13Chengdu buffer / optional Dujiangyan or Qingcheng MountainWeather, transport and energy buffer dayChengdu
Day 14Depart from ChengduDeparture day, no distant scenic area-

3. Core Price Reference Table (RMB + USD)

AreaAttraction / ItemRMBUSDNotes
ChengduChengdu Research Base of Giant Panda BreedingApprox. RMB 55Approx. USD 8Light nature/animal-themed buffer activity; best in the morning.
ChengduMetro / short city transportRMB 2-15USD 0.3-2Use Alipay/WeChat transit code or buy a single-ride ticket on site.
Chengdu East - Huanglongjiuzhai StationHigh-speed rail / EMU trainApprox. RMB 100-250USD 14-35Use real-time 12306 fare. Foreign visitors can buy with passport information.
Huanglongjiuzhai Station - JiuzhaigouOfficial shuttle / shared car / private carRMB 60-600USD 8-83Shuttle is cheaper; private car is more stable. About 2 hours.
JiuzhaigouPeak-season ticket + sightseeing busRMB 190 + 90USD 26 + 13April 1-November 15; official scenic-area price.
JiuzhaigouLow-season ticket + sightseeing busRMB 80 + 80USD 11 + 11November 16-March 31; official scenic-area price.
HuanglongEntrance + cableway / shuttle combinationApprox. RMB 170 + 20-120Approx. USD 24 + 3-17Check official/on-site price. Recommended: cableway up, walk down.
Chuanzhusi / SongpanLocal taxi / transferRMB 50-300USD 7-42Depends on hotel, station, airport and Huanglong transfer.
Chengdu - Mount SiguniangPrivate car / shared car / busRMB 150-1,200USD 21-167Private car is best for international visitors and families.
Shuangqiao ValleyTicket + sightseeing busRMB 80 + 70USD 11 + 10Official Mount Siguniang price reference. Best valley for most visitors.
Changping ValleyTicket + sightseeing busRMB 70 + 20USD 10 + 3More walking and light hiking.
Haizi ValleyTicketRMB 60USD 8No sightseeing bus for the main hiking route; best for stronger walkers.
Mount Siguniang Town - ChengduPrivate car / shared car / busRMB 150-1,200USD 21-167Return during daylight to reduce mountain-road risk.
Chengdu buffer dayDujiangyan / Qingcheng Mountain / city restRMB 0-150USD 0-21Optional. Use as a weather and energy buffer.
Price note: Scenic tickets, cableways, sightseeing buses, rail fares, private-car costs and hotel rates can change according to season, holidays, platform bundles and temporary policies. Use this table for route budgeting and check again before purchase.

4. Main Scenic Highlights and Backup Plans

Attraction / AreaPriorityWhat Is Most Worth SeeingBackup / Shortened Plan
Chengdu Panda BaseMost Worth SeeingActive morning panda behavior, young panda areas and bamboo-lined walking paths.If crowded or short on time, replace with People’s Park or Wangjianglou Park as a light buffer.
Jiuzhaigou - Five-Flower LakeMust SeeOne of Jiuzhaigou’s most representative lakes, with layered blue-green water, lake-bottom textures and strong autumn reflections.If crowded, take wide-angle photos first and move on toward Pearl Shoal.
Jiuzhaigou - Pearl Shoal WaterfallMust SeeWater spreads across a travertine shoal before dropping into the waterfall; excellent for dynamic water scenery.In rain or slippery conditions, shorten the boardwalk section.
Jiuzhaigou - Nuorilang WaterfallMust SeeOne of the park’s landmark waterfalls and a key transfer node between the three valleys.If energy is low, keep Nuorilang and reduce Shuzheng Valley walking.
Jiuzhaigou - Long Lake / Five-Color PondHighly RecommendedZechawa Valley’s high-lake combination. Long Lake is open and broad; Five-Color Pond is small but has concentrated color.If time is tight, prioritize Five-Color Pond over a long stay at Long Lake.
Huanglong - Five-Color PondMust SeeThe core pool cluster of Huanglong and the main target of the entire Huanglong visit. High altitude requires pacing.If altitude discomfort appears, use the cableway and see the essential Five-Color Pond area only.
Huanglong - Golden Sand Pavement / Zhengyan PoolsHighly RecommendedTravertine stream terraces and layered pools showing the transition of Huanglong’s landform.See them naturally while descending. Do not climb back and forth just to complete every point.
Shuangqiao Valley - Redwood ForestMust SeeThe endpoint of the sightseeing-bus route, with high-elevation forest and snow-mountain views.If weather is poor, stay briefly and move down to lower viewpoints.
Shuangqiao Valley - Sigu Nacuo / LongzhucuoMust SeeLake reflections, dead trees, water color and snow mountains form the most accessible classic Shuangqiao views.If tired, keep Sigu Nacuo and skip longer side walks.
Changping ValleyHighly RecommendedForests, streams, boardwalks and valley views make it the best light-hiking day in Mount Siguniang.Walk only to Kushu Tan / lower valley sections if energy is limited.
Haizi ValleyOptional AdvancedOpen snow-mountain viewpoints and higher hiking intensity; better for photographers and stronger walkers.Replace with Maobiliang viewpoint or a full rest day for ordinary visitors.
Dujiangyan / Qingcheng MountainOptional BufferGood final-buffer options near Chengdu, combining natural scenery with easy access.If tired or rainy, stay in Chengdu for rest, laundry, packing and a final meal.

5. Accommodation Guide: Location, Foreign-Visitor Convenience and Reasons to Stay

DestinationRecommended Stay TypeLocationWhy RecommendedForeign-Visitor ConvenienceReference Average Price
ChengduLuxury: The Temple House, Niccolo, St. Regis, InterContinental-style hotelsChunxi Road / Taikoo Li / Tianfu SquareClose to dining, shopping and metro. Foreign-guest check-in is smoother. Good for first and final nights.HighRMB 1,200-3,000 / USD 167-417
ChengduComfort: Atour / JI / Hampton-style hotels near Chengdu EastChengdu East Railway Station areaBest for early train departure and less morning pressure. Confirm passport check-in before booking.Medium-HighRMB 350-800 / USD 49-111
ChengduAirport-area chain hotelsShuangliu Airport / Tianfu AirportGood for late arrivals or early departures, not ideal for city sightseeing.Medium-HighRMB 300-800 / USD 42-111
JiuzhaigouLuxury: Hilton Jiuzhaigou, Banyan Tree-style resortsZhangzha Town / resort areaGood for families and higher budgets; breakfast and car arrangements are usually more stable. Check exact distance to the park entrance.HighRMB 900-2,500 / USD 125-347
JiuzhaigouComfort hotels near park entrance / Zhangzha TownNear Jiuzhaigou Visitor CenterBest for two days inside the park; shorter morning transfer and more dinner choices.Medium-HighRMB 350-1,000 / USD 49-139
JiuzhaigouBudget inns / guesthousesZhangzha Town or outer entrance areaLower cost, but confirm passport check-in, heating, breakfast and transport to the visitor center.Confirm before bookingRMB 180-500 / USD 25-69
Chuanzhusi / SongpanComfort / oxygen-support hotelsChuanzhusi Town or Songpan Ancient TownGood transition before or after Huanglong. Prioritize heating, oxygen, elevator and hot water.Confirm before bookingRMB 250-800 / USD 35-111
Mount SiguniangView hotels / better guesthousesMount Siguniang Town / Rilong TownClose to valley entrances. Good for Shuangqiao, Changping and rest days. Confirm heating and hot water.Confirm before bookingRMB 300-1,200 / USD 42-167
Mount SiguniangBudget guesthousesTown center / along main roadBudget option; confirm foreign passport registration, heating and car pickup point before payment.Confirm before bookingRMB 150-450 / USD 21-63
Chengdu final nightsComfort or luxury city hotelsChunxi Road, Tianfu Square, Chengdu East or airportUse Chengdu as the recovery and departure buffer after highland travel.HighRMB 350-3,000 / USD 49-417
Accommodation note: International visitors usually need their original passport to check in at hotels in China. Chengdu, higher-star hotels and international brands usually handle foreign guests more smoothly. Small inns in Jiuzhaigou, Chuanzhusi, Songpan and Mount Siguniang should be contacted before payment to confirm passport check-in, heating, hot water, elevators and oxygen availability.

6. Dining Guide: Specific Restaurants, Rating References, Specialties and Reasons to Choose

PlaceRestaurant / Food TypeRating ReferenceSpecialtyWhy RecommendedAverage Cost
ChengduChen Mapo Tofu (Luomashi / Qingyang branches)Approx. 4.2-4.6Mapo tofu, twice-cooked pork, classic Sichuan stir-friesA classic Sichuan restaurant suitable for a first local dinner.RMB 70-130 / USD 10-18
ChengduChengdu Chike (Kuixinglou / Zhimin Road branches)Approx. 4.3-4.7Creative Sichuan dishes, snack platters, spicy dishesRecognizable dishes and strong local identity; good for visitors wanting a popular Chengdu meal.RMB 100-180 / USD 14-25
ChengduMawangzi Sichuan Bistro (Taikoo Li / IFS area)Approx. 4.3-4.7Refined Sichuan cuisine, kung pao chicken, boiled dishesMore stable environment and service; suitable for foreign visitors and formal dinners.RMB 120-220 / USD 17-31
ChengduTaode Clay Pot (Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li area)Approx. 4.2-4.6Clay-pot dishes, home-style Sichuan foodWide menu and manageable spice level; good for families and groups.RMB 80-150 / USD 11-21
ChengduShudaxia / Dalongyi / Xiaolongkan Hotpot (choose branch near hotel)Approx. 4.1-4.6Sichuan hotpot, tripe, beef, duck bloodGood for visitors who want hotpot. Choose split broth if not used to spicy food.RMB 120-220 / USD 17-31
ChengduZhong Dumplings / Longchaoshou / Lai Tangyuan snack shopsApprox. 4.0-4.5Wontons, dandan noodles, sweet dumplingsUseful for light meals or transfer days, not high-end dining.RMB 25-60 / USD 3-8
Jiuzhaigou entranceAbuluzi Tibetan Restaurant / yak beef hotpot restaurantsApprox. 4.1-4.6Yak beef soup pot, butter tea, barley cakesGood Tibetan-style experience. Soup pot works well for groups.RMB 90-180 / USD 13-25
Jiuzhaigou entranceZhangzha Town Sichuan restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Home-style Sichuan dishes, noodles, rice mealsStable choice after a long park day; choose less spicy dishes if tired.RMB 60-120 / USD 8-17
Jiuzhaigou entranceHotel restaurants / resort restaurantsApprox. 4.1+Chinese / Tibetan / Western-style mealsMost reliable if arriving late, tired or traveling with children.RMB 100-250 / USD 14-35
Chuanzhusi / SongpanChuanzhusi yak beef soup pot restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Yak beef hotpot, hot soup, Sichuan-Tibetan dishesGood after Huanglong, when warm and easy-to-digest food is preferred.RMB 80-160 / USD 11-22
SongpanSongpan Ancient Town local restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Noodles, Sichuan dishes, yak meat dishesGood for one-night transition and a simple walk around town.RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17
Mount Siguniang TownRilong / town yak beef soup pot restaurantsApprox. 4.1-4.6Yak beef soup pot, mushroom soup, local hot dishesWarm soup is practical in high-altitude evenings.RMB 80-160 / USD 11-22
Mount Siguniang TownHiker cafes / hotel restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Coffee, simple Western food, noodles, riceGood for rest day or travelers who want less spicy food.RMB 50-150 / USD 7-21
Dujiangyan / Return corridorDujiangyan local Sichuan restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Sichuan dishes, river fish, noodlesUseful if returning to Chengdu via Dujiangyan or taking a buffer day.RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21
Dining note: Rating ranges are common references from public platforms such as Dianping, Ctrip and Trip.com. Branches, seasons and platform standards vary. Before departure, search the Chinese name again to verify operating status, ratings and queue conditions. Avoid alcohol before high-altitude days and prioritize warm soup, easy digestion and enough carbohydrates.

7. Intercity Transport and Public Transport Usage

SegmentBest OptionTimeReference PriceSuitable ForUsage / Notes
Chengdu airport -> city hotelMetro / taxi / hotel pickup45-90 minRMB 20-250 / USD 3-35All visitorsFor late arrival, use taxi or pickup. Save the hotel Chinese name.
Chengdu hotel -> Chengdu East Railway StationMetro / taxi20-60 minRMB 5-80 / USD 1-11Rail passengersUse Alipay/WeChat transit code or buy a metro ticket on site.
Chengdu East -> Huanglongjiuzhai StationHigh-speed rail / EMUApprox. 1.5-2.5 hrsApprox. RMB 100-250 / USD 14-35Jiuzhaigou visitorsBuy via 12306/Trip.com with passport information. Carry the original passport and use staffed/passport channels if needed.
Huanglongjiuzhai Station -> Jiuzhaigou entranceOfficial shuttle / shared car / private carApprox. 2 hrsRMB 60-600 / USD 8-83All visitorsUse official/regular shuttles after arrival. If booking private car, confirm destination as Jiuzhaigou entrance / Zhangzha Town.
Jiuzhaigou entrance -> visitor centerWalk / hotel shuttle / short transfer5-25 minRMB 0-50 / USD 0-7Park visitorsStay near the entrance if visiting twice. Do not schedule the morning too tightly.
Jiuzhaigou -> HuanglongPrivate car / shared car2-3 hrsRMB 100-800 / USD 14-111Day 6 visitorsAltitude rises during this segment. Avoid alcohol and strenuous activity.
Huanglong -> Chuanzhusi / SongpanPrivate car / shared car0.5-1.5 hrsRMB 80-300 / USD 11-42After HuanglongDo not rush a night return to Chengdu after Huanglong.
Chuanzhusi / Songpan -> ChengduRail / flight / private transfer1.5-8 hrsRMB 100-1,200 / USD 14-167Return to ChengduRail depends on station and timetable. Private car is longer and more tiring.
Chengdu -> Mount Siguniang TownPrivate car / shared car / bus4.5-6 hrsRMB 150-1,200 / USD 21-167All visitorsPrivate car is safest and clearest for international travelers with luggage.
Mount Siguniang Town -> Shuangqiao ValleyTaxi / hotel car / scenic transport15-30 minRMB 20-100 / USD 3-14Day 9 visitorsThe valley uses sightseeing buses internally.
Mount Siguniang Town -> Changping ValleyTaxi / hotel car / short transfer10-25 minRMB 20-80 / USD 3-11Day 10 visitorsPrepare for more walking than Shuangqiao.
Mount Siguniang Town -> ChengduPrivate car / bus4.5-6 hrsRMB 150-1,200 / USD 21-167All visitorsReturn in daylight to reduce mountain-road weather risk.
Public transport usage for international visitors: For high-speed rail, use 12306, Trip.com or station ticket counters. The passport used for booking must match the passport used for travel. If an automatic gate does not read a passport, use the staffed gate. For Chengdu metro, use Alipay/WeChat transit codes or buy single-ride tickets. For scenic shuttles and coaches, keep the Chinese boarding point, driver phone number, vehicle plate and destination name. For long mountain-road segments, confirm the driver, vehicle, price, tolls, parking fees and hotel pickup/drop-off one day in advance. Avoid unlicensed tout vehicles.

8. Day-by-Day Detailed Travel Guide

Day 1 | Arrival in Chengdu

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusArrival, hotel check-in, payment and network check. Do not schedule intensive sightseeing.
RouteDeparture city -> Chengdu
OvernightChengdu
Estimated CostRMB 500-2,500 / USD 69-347
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
After arrivalAirport / railway station to hotel45-90 minUse taxi or hotel pickup. First-time international visitors should not take complicated transfers with luggage.
16:00-18:00Hotel check-in and rest1-2 hrsCheck passport, visa page, hotel booking, Alipay, WeChat Pay, international bank card, cash and mobile data.
18:30-20:00First Chengdu dinner1-1.5 hrsIf staying near Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li, choose Chen Mapo Tofu, Taode Clay Pot or a mall restaurant. If not used to spicy food, choose hotel dining.
After 20:00Short walk or direct rest30-60 minStay close to the hotel. Do not schedule long-distance night transport.

Scenic Focus: Chengdu is not the natural climax of the route. It is the transport and adaptation base. The most important tasks today are a smooth arrival, functioning mobile payment and enough rest.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If the flight is delayed or arrival is late at night, cancel all evening activities and go straight to the hotel. If mobile payment fails, use cash first or ask the hotel front desk for help.

Dining Suggestions: Dinner: Chen Mapo Tofu, Taode Clay Pot, Chengdu Chike, or hotel restaurant. Budget: RMB 70-180 / USD 10-25.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay near Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li, Tianfu Square, Chengdu East Railway Station or the airport. For an early train next day, choose Chengdu East; for food convenience, choose Chunxi Road / Taikoo Li.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Save the hotel Chinese name, address and phone number. Use official taxi or hotel pickup for late-night arrivals. Prepare rail tickets, scenic tickets and the original passport for later days.

Day 2 | Chengdu Buffer and Light Nature Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusPanda Base and city supplies before entering higher-altitude areas.
RouteChengdu city
OvernightChengdu
Estimated CostRMB 650-2,800 / USD 90-389
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
07:30-08:30Breakfast and departure1 hrPandas are usually more active in the morning, so leave early.
08:30-11:30Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding2.5-3 hrsFocus on adult panda activity areas, young panda areas and bamboo paths. Avoid arriving around noon when pandas may rest.
11:30-13:00Return downtown for lunch1-1.5 hrsChoose Chengdu Chike, Taode Clay Pot or a mall restaurant.
14:00-16:00People’s Park / Wangjianglou / hotel rest1-2 hrsUse this as a light city buffer. Do not walk too much.
16:00-18:00Supplies and transport confirmation1-2 hrsBuy sunscreen, warm layer, non-slip shoes and motion-sickness medicine. Confirm Day 3 rail ticket, passport and station transfer.
18:30-20:00Formal Sichuan dinner1.5 hrsChoose Mawangzi, Chen Mapo Tofu or hotpot. Do not drink alcohol before high-altitude travel.

Scenic Focus: The Panda Base is the best light natural opening for Chengdu. It is recognizable, easy to execute and not too physically demanding.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If you do not want to visit the Panda Base, replace it with Chengdu Museum, People’s Park or Wangjianglou Park. Families with children can do only half a day and then return to the hotel.

Dining Suggestions: Lunch: Chengdu Chike or Taode Clay Pot. Dinner: Chen Mapo Tofu, Mawangzi or hotpot. Budget: RMB 80-220 / USD 11-31.

Accommodation Suggestions: Continue staying in Chengdu. Prioritize a hotel convenient for Chengdu East Railway Station if taking an early train next day.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Do not drink alcohol before high-altitude travel. Prepare warm clothes, rain gear and sunscreen. Make sure the passport number on the 12306/Trip.com rail order matches the actual passport.

Day 3 | Chengdu East - Huanglongjiuzhai Station - Jiuzhaigou Entrance

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusEnter northwest Sichuan by rail and rest near Jiuzhaigou.
RouteChengdu East -> Huanglongjiuzhai Station -> Jiuzhaigou entrance / Zhangzha Town
OvernightJiuzhaigou entrance
Estimated CostRMB 900-3,500 / USD 125-486
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
07:00-08:00Go to Chengdu East Railway Station40-60 minUse taxi or direct metro. Carry original passport and use staffed/passport gate if needed.
08:00-11:00Chengdu East to Huanglongjiuzhai StationApprox. 1.5-2.5 hrsTrain time depends on the actual 12306 timetable. Rest on the train and avoid overexertion.
11:00-13:30Huanglongjiuzhai Station to Jiuzhaigou entranceApprox. 2 hrsAfter arrival, use official shuttle, shared car or pre-booked private car. Confirm destination as Jiuzhaigou entrance / Zhangzha Town hotel.
13:30-15:00Hotel check-in and lunch1.5 hrsCheck in first, then have a simple lunch. Do not enter the park today.
15:00-17:30Entrance-area adaptation and ticket check1-2.5 hrsConfirm Day 4 ticket, sightseeing bus, entry time and walking/transfer time to the visitor center.
18:00-20:00Early dinner and rest1.5 hrsChoose Tibetan yak soup pot, Sichuan food or hotel restaurant. Sleep early.

Scenic Focus: Today’s goal is not sightseeing but a safe transition from Chengdu to Jiuzhaigou. The altitude and temperature are different from Chengdu, so leave time to adapt.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If rail tickets are sold out, consider flying to Jiuzhai Huanglong Airport and using private transfer to the entrance. If arrival is late, cancel the entrance-area walk and only have dinner and rest.

Dining Suggestions: Jiuzhaigou entrance: Abuluzi-style Tibetan food, yak beef soup pot, barbecue or hotel restaurant. Budget: RMB 70-180 / USD 10-25.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay near Jiuzhaigou Visitor Center / entrance / Zhangzha Town. The closer to the visitor center, the easier two-day park entry will be.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Jiuzhaigou requires real-name reservation and original valid identity document for entry. Do not enter the park immediately after arrival. Prepare a jacket for cold evenings.

Day 4 | Jiuzhaigou Core Route

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusFirst entry into Jiuzhaigou. Prioritize Rize Valley, Zechawa Valley and Nuorilang core sites.
RouteJiuzhaigou Visitor Center -> Rize Valley -> Nuorilang -> Zechawa Valley -> Shuzheng highlights
OvernightJiuzhaigou entrance
Estimated CostRMB 900-3,300 / USD 125-458
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
07:00-08:00Breakfast and go to visitor center1 hrBring passport, ticket order, sunscreen, raincoat and water. Enter early.
08:00-09:00Enter and take sightseeing bus toward upper Rize ValleyApprox. 1 hrFollow scenic-area dispatching and head to the core Rize Valley direction first.
09:00-10:00Arrow Bamboo Lake / Panda Lake area40-60 minSee highland lakes, forest reflections and morning light. Shorten this stop if time is limited.
10:00-11:00Five-Flower Lake45-60 minOne of Jiuzhaigou’s most important lakes, with layered water colors, autumn forests and lake-bottom patterns.
11:00-12:00Pearl Shoal Waterfall45-60 minWalk along the travertine shoal and waterfall. Slow down if the boardwalk is wet.
12:00-13:00Nuorilang Center lunch / rest1 hrFood choices are limited. Bring snacks if preferred.
13:00-14:30Long Lake + Five-Color Pond1-1.5 hrsCore Zechawa Valley sites. Five-Color Pond is small but color is concentrated and highly photogenic.
14:30-16:30Nuorilang Waterfall + Shuzheng highlights1.5-2 hrsKeep Nuorilang as a priority and add Shuzheng lakes according to energy.
16:30-17:30Exit park and return to hotel1 hrAvoid leaving too late during peak season.
18:00-20:00Dinner and rest1.5 hrsHot soup or hotel dining is recommended after a long walking day.

Scenic Focus: Five-Flower Lake, Pearl Shoal Waterfall, Long Lake, Five-Color Pond and Nuorilang Waterfall are the key sites to keep on the first day.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If entry is late, prioritize Five-Flower Lake, Pearl Shoal, Five-Color Pond and Nuorilang. If raining, reduce boardwalk walking and use sightseeing buses more.

Dining Suggestions: Lunch at Nuorilang Center or bring light food. Dinner near the entrance: yak beef soup pot / Sichuan food / hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Continue staying near the entrance to avoid moving hotels before the second park day.

Foreign Visitor Notes: The scenic area is large and must be visited by sightseeing bus. Stay on boardwalks and enter early during autumn peak season.

Day 5 | Second Day in Jiuzhaigou / Low-Intensity Photography Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusSecond entry into Jiuzhaigou. Add Shuzheng Valley, repeat Five-Flower Lake, and adjust by energy.
RouteJiuzhaigou entrance -> Shuzheng Valley / Rize repeat -> entrance
OvernightJiuzhaigou entrance
Estimated CostRMB 900-3,300 / USD 125-458
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
07:30-08:30Breakfast and second entry1 hrThe second day can be slower than Day 4.
08:30-10:30Shuzheng Valley highlights1.5-2 hrsShuzheng Lakes, Wolong Lake, Sparkling Lake and Reed Lake are good for slower water and boardwalk photos.
10:30-12:00Mirror Lake / Five-Flower Lake repeat1-1.5 hrsIf the weather is good, revisit reflection points and wait for better light.
12:00-13:00Lunch / rest1 hrSelf-prepared light food saves time.
13:00-15:30Flexible add-on according to energy2-2.5 hrsAdd Panda Lake, Nuorilang or Long Lake if energy is good. Do not force every site.
15:30-17:00Exit early and rest1-1.5 hrsSave energy for Huanglong high-altitude walking on Day 6.
18:00-20:00Dinner and luggage preparation1.5 hrsConfirm Day 6 private car/shared car departure time.

Scenic Focus: The value of the second day is slower pacing. Shuzheng Valley’s connected lakes and boardwalks are better for relaxed viewing than the first core day.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If tired, do only a half-day entry and leave after lunch. If weather is poor, prioritize closer Shuzheng Valley sites instead of long transfers inside the park.

Dining Suggestions: Lunch: self-prepared food or simple park meal. Dinner: Tibetan soup pot or hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay near Jiuzhaigou entrance again. Do not change hotels before a transfer day.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Keep walking intensity moderate. Prepare warm clothing and rain gear for Huanglong.

Day 6 | Jiuzhaigou - Huanglong - Chuanzhusi / Songpan

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusVisit Huanglong core travertine pools and overnight in Chuanzhusi/Songpan as a buffer.
RouteJiuzhaigou -> Huanglong -> Chuanzhusi / Songpan
OvernightChuanzhusi or Songpan
Estimated CostRMB 900-3,500 / USD 125-486
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
07:00-08:00Breakfast and departure to Huanglong1 hrBring warm clothing, sunscreen, raincoat, water and light food.
08:00-10:30Jiuzhaigou to Huanglong2-2.5 hrsMountain transfer with rising altitude. Avoid alcohol and strenuous activity.
10:30-11:30Cableway / shuttle upward30-60 minRecommended: take cableway up to reduce high-altitude climbing.
11:30-13:00Five-Color Pond1-1.5 hrsHuanglong’s key site. Focus on layered pool colors and snow-mountain background.
13:00-15:30Huanglong Temple, Zhengyan Pools, Golden Sand Pavement, Bonsai Pools downhill2-2.5 hrsWalk downhill to see travertine terraces and pool layers. Shorten route if energy is low.
15:30-17:00Leave Huanglong for Chuanzhusi / Songpan0.5-1.5 hrsDo not travel too far tonight.
18:00-20:00Hot dinner and rest1.5 hrsChoose yak beef soup pot or Sichuan food and sleep early.

Scenic Focus: Five-Color Pond is the target point of Huanglong. Golden Sand Pavement and Zhengyan Pools show the gradual change of the travertine landform.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If altitude discomfort appears, use the cableway and see only the Five-Color Pond highlights. If symptoms are strong, skip Huanglong and rest in Chuanzhusi/Songpan.

Dining Suggestions: Lunch: simple meal or packed food. Dinner: yak beef soup pot / Sichuan food in Chuanzhusi or Songpan.

Accommodation Suggestions: Choose Chuanzhusi or Songpan hotels with heating, hot water and oxygen support if possible.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Huanglong is higher than Jiuzhaigou. Walk slowly, avoid running, and do not drink alcohol.

Day 7 | Chuanzhusi / Songpan - Chengdu

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusReturn from the highland area to Chengdu and recover.
RouteChuanzhusi / Songpan -> Huanglongjiuzhai Station / Songpan Station -> Chengdu East
OvernightChengdu
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,800 / USD 97-389
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:00-09:00Breakfast and checkout1 hrDepart according to train/flight timing; do not cut it too close.
09:00-11:00Go to station / airport / transport node0.5-1.5 hrsConfirm transfer time from hotel to Huanglongjiuzhai Station / Songpan Station.
11:00-14:00Return to ChengduApprox. 1.5-3 hrs by rail / depends on transportUse actual train/flight schedule.
14:00-16:00Chengdu hotel check-in and rest1-2 hrsAfter returning from high altitude, avoid intensive sightseeing.
17:00-20:00Dinner, laundry and supplies2-3 hrsWash clothes, restock snacks and medicine, and prepare for Mount Siguniang.

Scenic Focus: This is a recovery and logistics day. Returning to Chengdu reduces altitude pressure and gives the route a safe reset before Mount Siguniang.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If return tickets are unavailable, stay one more night in Songpan/Chuanzhusi and adjust the Chengdu buffer. Do not force a late-night mountain-road return.

Dining Suggestions: Dinner in Chengdu: Taode Clay Pot, Mawangzi, hotpot or hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay in Chengdu. If departing early for Mount Siguniang on Day 8, choose a hotel with convenient pickup.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Confirm Day 8 driver/vehicle and weather. Prepare motion-sickness medicine and warm clothing.

Day 8 | Chengdu - Mount Siguniang Town

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusLong mountain-road transfer and altitude adaptation in Mount Siguniang Town.
RouteChengdu -> Dujiangyan / Yingxiu / Wolong -> Mount Siguniang Town
OvernightMount Siguniang Town
Estimated CostRMB 900-3,200 / USD 125-444
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:00-09:00Breakfast and departure1 hrPrivate car is recommended. Driver picks up at the hotel. Bring jacket, motion-sickness medicine and snacks.
09:00-12:00Chengdu toward WolongApprox. 3 hrsPass Dujiangyan, Yingxiu and Wolong direction. Short toilet/photo stops are acceptable.
12:00-13:00Lunch on the road1 hrChoose simple food; avoid very oily meals.
13:00-15:30Wolong / Balang Mountain direction to Mount Siguniang Town2-2.5 hrsRoad has curves and weather changes quickly.
15:30-17:00Hotel check-in and altitude adaptation1-1.5 hrsDo not immediately do strenuous walking after arrival.
17:00-18:30Maobiliang viewpoint if weather and energy allow30-60 minGood distant view of Mount Siguniang. Cancel if tired.
18:30-20:00Dinner and early rest1.5 hrsWarm soup pot or Sichuan food is recommended.

Scenic Focus: The transfer itself introduces western Sichuan mountain scenery. Maobiliang is the easiest optional viewpoint if weather is good.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If road conditions are poor or arrival is late, cancel Maobiliang and rest in town. If prone to carsickness, sit in front and avoid heavy lunch.

Dining Suggestions: Lunch: simple road meal. Dinner: yak beef soup pot or town restaurant, RMB 80-160 / USD 11-22.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay in Mount Siguniang Town / Rilong Town. Confirm heating, hot water, oxygen support and passport check-in before booking.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Altitude is higher than Chengdu. Walk slowly after arrival and do not drink alcohol.

Day 9 | Shuangqiao Valley

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusThe most accessible Mount Siguniang valley, with sightseeing buses, snow mountains, lakes, meadows and redwood forest.
RouteMount Siguniang Town -> Shuangqiao Valley -> Mount Siguniang Town
OvernightMount Siguniang Town
Estimated CostRMB 650-2,400 / USD 90-333
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:00-09:00Breakfast and go to Shuangqiao Valley Visitor Center1 hrBring passport, ticket order, warm clothing and sunscreen.
09:00-10:00Take sightseeing bus to Redwood ForestApprox. 1 hrRecommended method: ride to the endpoint first, then visit stops while coming down.
10:00-10:50Redwood Forest40-50 minHigh-altitude forest, snow mountains and valley-end scenery. Very photogenic in clear weather.
11:00-11:40Potala Peak / Hunter Peak / Eagle Beak Rock viewpoints30-40 minSee mountain shapes and glacial landforms.
11:40-12:30Simple lunch / packed food40-60 minFood options are limited. Bring water and energy food.
12:30-13:30Sigu Nacuo45-60 minLake reflections, dead trees and snow mountains are among the valley’s best combinations.
13:30-14:30Longzhucuo / Science Base45-60 minWater color, travertine deposits and ecological boardwalks.
14:30-16:00Nianyuba / meadow sections1-1.5 hrsSee open valley scenery and take easy walks.
16:00-17:00Return to town1 hrRest after returning.
18:00-20:00Dinner1.5 hrsWarm soup or hotel restaurant.

Scenic Focus: Shuangqiao Valley is the most suitable Mount Siguniang valley for ordinary visitors. The sightseeing bus reduces physical pressure while still giving snow-mountain, lake and meadow views.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If weather is poor, keep only Redwood Forest, Sigu Nacuo and one mid-valley viewpoint. If altitude discomfort appears, stay on the bus more and reduce walking.

Dining Suggestions: Bring lunch/snacks into the valley. Dinner in town: yak beef soup pot, noodles or hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Continue staying in Mount Siguniang Town. Avoid changing hotels during the valley days.

Foreign Visitor Notes: The valley is high-altitude. Keep warm and use sunscreen. Do not run after buses or walk fast.

Day 10 | Changping Valley

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusForest, streams, boardwalks and light hiking in a snow-mountain valley.
RouteMount Siguniang Town -> Changping Valley -> Mount Siguniang Town
OvernightMount Siguniang Town
Estimated CostRMB 650-2,500 / USD 90-347
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:00-09:00Breakfast and go to Changping Valley Visitor Center1 hrToday involves more walking. Wear non-slip shoes.
09:00-09:30Sightseeing bus to Lama Temple areaApprox. 30 minTicket and sightseeing bus are counted separately.
09:30-10:15Lama Temple area30-45 minSee the forested valley entrance and use it as the hiking start point.
10:15-12:00Boardwalk toward Kushu Tan / lower valley1.5-2 hrsBest light-hiking section for most visitors, with forest, streams and snow-mountain angles.
12:00-13:00Lunch / rest1 hrBring light food and water.
13:00-15:00Continue toward Muluozi direction or return2 hrsStrong walkers may continue; ordinary visitors should turn back before energy drops.
15:00-16:30Return to entrance and town1-1.5 hrsDo not exit too late.
18:00-20:00Dinner and rest1.5 hrsEat warm and easy-to-digest food.

Scenic Focus: Changping Valley is more immersive than Shuangqiao. It is best for forest, stream and valley walking. It should be treated as a light hike, not a bus-tour day.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If energy is low, walk only the boardwalk near Lama Temple and Kushu Tan. If raining or snowy, reduce walking and return earlier.

Dining Suggestions: Bring packed lunch or snacks. Dinner: town soup pot / hotel meal.

Accommodation Suggestions: Continue staying in Mount Siguniang Town.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Do not attempt long hikes without preparation. Weather changes quickly; carry rain gear and a warm layer.

Day 11 | Haizi Valley / Maobiliang / Rest Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusChoose snow-mountain viewpoints, short hiking or full rest according to energy.
RouteAround Mount Siguniang Town
OvernightMount Siguniang Town
Estimated CostRMB 450-2,000 / USD 63-278
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:30-09:30Breakfast and energy check1 hrThis day is flexible. Decide based on fatigue after Days 9-10.
09:30-12:00Option A: Maobiliang + easy town photos2-2.5 hrsBest for ordinary visitors. See distant views and the line of the four peaks.
09:00-14:00Option B: Short Haizi Valley route3-5 hrsFor photographers and stronger walkers. Walk toward Guozhuangping / Chaoshanping. Do not push deep toward Dahaizi unless prepared.
Morning/afternoonOption C: Full restHalf day or full daySleep in, café, laundry and photo sorting. Good preparation for return to Chengdu.
16:00-18:00Confirm Day 12 transport30-60 minConfirm driver/coach, departure time, weather and road conditions.
18:00-20:00Final dinner in the mountain town1.5 hrsChoose warm soup pot or easy-to-digest food.

Scenic Focus: Haizi Valley has more open snow-mountain views but requires stronger walking ability. For most visitors, Maobiliang plus rest is safer and still scenic.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If tired, choose a complete rest day. If weather is bad, do not enter Haizi Valley; stay in town and prepare for return to Chengdu.

Dining Suggestions: Town cafés, hotel restaurants, yak beef soup pot or simple noodles. Budget: RMB 50-160 / USD 7-22.

Accommodation Suggestions: Continue staying in the same town hotel/guesthouse.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Do not underestimate Haizi Valley. It is not the default route for ordinary visitors.

Day 12 | Mount Siguniang Town - Chengdu

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusReturn to Chengdu during the day as a recovery and city buffer.
RouteMount Siguniang Town -> Chengdu
OvernightChengdu
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,800 / USD 97-389
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
08:00-09:00Breakfast and checkout1 hrConfirm luggage, driver and return route.
09:00-14:30Mount Siguniang Town to Chengdu5-5.5 hrsPrivate car is the most stable option. Short stops are fine, but do not add a major scenic area.
14:30-16:00Chengdu hotel check-in and rest1-1.5 hrsRest after returning from high altitude.
16:00-18:00Laundry / supplies / free time1-2 hrsHandle clothes, photos and travel supplies.
18:30-20:30Chengdu dinner1.5-2 hrsChoose a comfortable Sichuan meal or hotpot, but avoid excessive alcohol.

Scenic Focus: The main value is safety and recovery. Daylight return reduces mountain-road risk and leaves time to recover in Chengdu.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If the road is affected by snow or heavy rain, leave earlier or postpone the return. Do not drive late at night on mountain roads.

Dining Suggestions: Dinner: Mawangzi, Taode Clay Pot, hotpot or hotel/mall restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay in Chengdu. Choose Chunxi Road/Taikoo Li for dinner convenience or airport/station area for departure logistics.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Keep this day simple. Do not add Dujiangyan or Qingcheng Mountain unless the itinerary is running very smoothly.

Day 13 | Chengdu Buffer / Optional Dujiangyan or Qingcheng Mountain

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusWeather, transport and energy buffer day, with optional light nature sightseeing.
RouteChengdu surroundings or Chengdu city
OvernightChengdu
Estimated CostRMB 500-2,500 / USD 69-347
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
09:00-10:00Sleep in and breakfast1 hrDo not start too early unless going to Dujiangyan / Qingcheng Mountain.
10:00-16:00Option A: Dujiangyan easy visit4-6 hrsSee the irrigation system, riverside paths and Nanqiao area. Good for nature plus engineering.
10:00-16:30Option B: Qingcheng Mountain front mountain4-6 hrsGood for forest paths and Taoist mountain scenery. Stone steps can be slippery in rain.
All dayOption C: Chengdu restHalf day or full dayPeople’s Park, Wangjianglou, malls, cafés, laundry and souvenirs.
18:00-20:30Final dinner1.5-2 hrsChoose Sichuan cuisine, hotpot or a stable hotel/mall restaurant.

Scenic Focus: This day protects the whole route from weather, road delays and accumulated fatigue. It is intentionally flexible.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If tired or rainy, stay in Chengdu and rest. If the route was delayed earlier, use today to absorb the delay instead of adding sightseeing.

Dining Suggestions: Final Chengdu dinner: Chen Mapo Tofu, Chengdu Chike, Mawangzi, hotpot, or hotel restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions: Stay in Chengdu. Choose based on next-day departure airport/station.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Do not schedule a faraway scenic area before departure day.

Day 14 | Depart from Chengdu

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusDeparture day; only light activity or direct departure.
RouteChengdu -> next destination
Overnight-
Estimated CostRMB 100-700 / USD 14-97, excluding long-distance flight/rail ticket
TimePlanSuggested StayExecution Notes
MorningHotel breakfast and packing1 hrCheck passport, visa page, flight/rail order, chargers and personal items.
Depending on departure timeOptional light activity1-2 hrsChoose only a nearby walk, mall meal or café. Do not visit distant attractions.
Before departureTransfer to airport / railway station45-120 minArrive 3 hours early for international flights; 1-1.5 hours early for rail.

Scenic Focus: The final day is for smooth departure. Keeping it light prevents missed flights or trains.

Alternative / Low-Energy Plan: If departure is delayed, stay close to the airport or railway station.

Dining Suggestions: Breakfast at hotel. Lunch/dinner at a mall, airport or railway station depending on timing.

Accommodation Suggestions: No planned accommodation. If delayed, choose a chain hotel near the airport or station.

Foreign Visitor Notes: Confirm the exact airport/station in Chinese. Chengdu has multiple airports and railway stations.

9. Three-Level Budget Estimate (14 Days, Excluding International Flights)

LevelConfigurationTotal RMB BudgetTotal USD BudgetSuitable For and Notes
BudgetRegular hotels/guesthouses + public transport/coaches + simple mealsRMB 11,000-16,000USD 1,528-2,222Young travelers or visitors with China independent-travel experience; more transfers and lower comfort.
Comfort (Recommended)Comfort hotels + private cars for key segments + local restaurantsRMB 20,000-30,000USD 2,778-4,167Best for most international visitors; balances safety, comfort and execution difficulty.
High-EndHigh-end hotels/resorts + mostly private cars + selected restaurantsRMB 38,000-65,000USD 5,278-9,028Families, couples and higher-budget visitors; reduces language and transfer pressure.
CategoryBudgetComfortHigh-End
AccommodationRMB 5,000-8,000 / USD 694-1,111RMB 10,000-17,000 / USD 1,389-2,361RMB 25,000-45,000 / USD 3,472-6,250
Intercity Transport / Private CarsRMB 2,500-4,500 / USD 347-625RMB 5,000-8,000 / USD 694-1,111RMB 8,000-13,000 / USD 1,111-1,806
Tickets / Sightseeing BusesRMB 2,000-3,000 / USD 278-417RMB 2,500-3,500 / USD 347-486RMB 3,500-5,000 / USD 486-694
DiningRMB 1,500-3,000 / USD 208-417RMB 3,500-6,000 / USD 486-833RMB 7,000-12,000 / USD 972-1,667
Backup Cash / MiscellaneousRMB 500-1,500 / USD 69-208RMB 1,000-2,500 / USD 139-347RMB 2,000-5,000 / USD 278-694
Budget note: These are per-person reference budgets, excluding international flights, shopping and travel insurance. If two travelers share hotels and private cars, the per-person accommodation and transport cost may decrease. During Chinese public holidays, autumn peak season or high-end hotel sell-out periods, accommodation and private-car prices may rise significantly.

10. Practical Tips for International Visitors

Documents and ticketing: Carry your original passport with you. Hotel check-in, high-speed rail, scenic-area ticket windows and real-name reservation checks may require the physical passport. If an online ticketing page does not accept passports, try Trip.com, 12306, Ctrip international version, hotel front desk assistance or the scenic-area manual ticket counter.

Payment and cash: Use Alipay as the main payment tool and WeChat Pay as a backup. Carry Visa/Mastercard and RMB 800-2,000 in cash because mountain towns, small restaurants and drivers may rely more on cash or domestic payment methods.

Altitude and health: Jiuzhaigou, Huanglong and Mount Siguniang are highland areas. Walk slowly, avoid alcohol, keep warm and do not overfill the first high-altitude day. Travelers with heart disease, serious hypertension or respiratory conditions should consult a doctor before this route.

Language and navigation: Save all hotel, attraction, station and airport names in Chinese. Showing drivers Chinese names is more reliable than English names. Prepare phrases such as: “I need to buy a ticket with my passport,” “Please take me to this hotel,” and “Please help me call the driver.”

Luggage and gear: Bring non-slip shoes, raincoat, warm layers, sunscreen, sunglasses, power bank, motion-sickness medicine and basic stomach medicine. A raincoat is more practical than an umbrella in Jiuzhaigou, Huanglong and Mount Siguniang.

11. Risks and Backup Plans

RiskPossible ImpactBackup Plan
Altitude discomfortHeadache, shortness of breath or fatigue may occur in Huanglong, Mount Siguniang Town, Shuangqiao, Changping and Haizi valleys.Reduce walking, use cableway/sightseeing buses, cancel long Haizi Valley route, descend or seek medical help if needed.
Rain/snow/road conditionsMountain roads such as Chengdu-Mount Siguniang and Jiuzhaigou-Huanglong may be affected.Do not schedule attractions on transfer days. Return to Chengdu during daylight. Avoid night mountain roads in winter.
CrowdsJiuzhaigou, Panda Base and Huanglong can be crowded during peak season.Enter early, keep the core sites, and reduce non-core boardwalk sections.
Public transport ticketing failureForeign visitors may face online registration, passport recognition or sold-out ticket issues.Use 12306/Trip.com, carry passport to staffed ticket counters, and confirm key transfers 1-3 days ahead.
Restaurant changesSmall-town restaurants may change hours, ratings or availability.Search Chinese names on Dianping/Ctrip before departure and keep hotel restaurants/simple meals as backup.
Low energyBack-to-back park days and highland walking can accumulate fatigue.Use Day 5 as a half-day park visit; replace Haizi Valley with Maobiliang/rest; keep Day 13 as an insurance day.
Western Sichuan Nature Travel Guide · Full Guide · Responsive web edition
